title: Plug Depth
wikitext:
[]: Plug Depth             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Thu Dec 24, 2009 12:43 pm What does term plug depth (プラグ深度) exactly mean and how it connected to synchronization rate (シンクロ率)? Could it be an immediate physical regulator between Eva's core unit and it's pilot?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Dec 24, 2009 1:39 pm The term refers literally to how deep into the Eva's core the entry plug is inserted. Note how the plug goes thru the spinal chord right into where the core is located in the cross-section image on the right:

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 24, 2009 1:51 pm I love that image, get to see 02 from the front for a quick change. The deeper the plug goes, the better the sync but if you go to far you lose your lilim form and return to LCL within the core of the Unit. So its kinda like "Do good but not toooo good".

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Dec 24, 2009 11:14 pm eww thats just nasty? then why the hell they have such a big hunchback ?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 24, 2009 11:24 pm bladerj wrote: eww thats just nasty? then why the hell they have such a big hunchback ?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 24, 2009 11:34 pm The second Funimation Rebuild clip (which has little spoilers) has an interesting observation on this topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Fri Dec 25, 2009 1:18 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The second Funimation Rebuild clip (which has little spoilers) has an interesting observation on this topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Dec 25, 2009 7:39 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The second Funimation Rebuild clip (which has little spoilers) has an interesting observation on this topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Fri Dec 25, 2009 10:51 am That really does show just how large the Eva units are.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Dec 30, 2009 7:43 pm There are a few clues in the series about this - there's a scene where the three pilots faces are shown with various degrees of syncs, the images with the lower (further down) faces are more highly synced. I believe this is ep 12. There are also comments about "contamination" occuring at lower plug depths, such as when Shinji tests with 00 (ep 14); aldo a comment is made about Kaworu's plug depth being even further than Shinji's, into a danger zone (ep 24).

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 31, 2009 1:36 am Another side question, if that is Unit 02...Why do I only see one eye on one side?

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Thu Dec 31, 2009 1:47 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Another side question, if that is Unit 02...Why do I only see one eye on one side?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 31, 2009 1:48 am AVman9 wrote: I've always been under the impression that the "eyes" we see on the Eva units are actually part of the armor they wear. I think that they all have two eyes, but the armor is different on each unit. I have no idea if this is a legitimate possibility, but you never know....

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:00 am Sachi wrote: Nope.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:03 am What the hell are you two looking at? Please point it out to me, because I fail.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:15 am Sachi wrote: What the hell are you two looking at? Please point it out to me, because I fail.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:19 am And where do you see an eye? I highly doubt that round mark in the center is an eye. If that is a side shot from a complete 9 o'clock angle, the eye would not be right there.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:23 am Sachi wrote: And where do you see an eye? I highly doubt that round mark in the center is an eye. If that is a side shot from a complete 9 o'clock angle, the eye would not be right there.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 31, 2009 11:32 am Look below it at the other image, there is clearly an eye where a normal Human eye would be yet I don't see another one on that side.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Dec 31, 2009 2:20 pm This is pretty much certainly a single ocular cavity on the left side of the skull: ...and is probably an error. Ocular cavities in humans have rather heavy bone formation, so they should also show up in an "x-ray" of an Eva. Yes, all we see on the outside of the Eva armor are lenses, though it appears that the number of eye lenses we see does indeed match up to organic eyes of the Eva. So, the graphic the animator has provided does not match what we know about Eva 02.
